lines.translation: It has been two or three months since Zhang Tianming and his three friends came to Beijing, and the weather has gradually gotten warmer. Every day, they were busy with classes or job hunting and hadn't seen each other for half a month. Today is Saturday, and everyone wants to relax. Tianming suggested going mountain climbing. Lisha and Xuemei didn't object, and Ke Lin also thought it was a good idea, saying that his German friend Mark also wanted to go.

But how should they get there? Taking a taxi would be too expensive and boring; taking the bus would be too crowded with too many people. Ke Lin came up with an idea: ride bikes! Everyone immediately agreed, feeling that riding bikes is not only good exercise and saves money, but is also beneficial for environmental protection.
They set out very early in the morning. After riding for a long way, they got a bit hot and tired, so they got off their bikes, pushing them while they chatted.
The air here is better than in the city, and the smog isn't as bad.
It's green everywhere, which is really pleasant to look at!
Oh, I'm dying of thirst... Look, we've finished a whole bottle of water! Is there a recycling bin around here? We can't just throw the empty bottle anywhere.
Right, we should be mindful of the environment. The recycling bin... look, it's under the tree.
I haven't bought bottled water in a long time. Some classmates in our class suggested that to protect our green planet, we should bring our own water instead of buying bottled water.
That's a good suggestion. Hey, look, what are those shiny things on the roof of that building over there?
Are those solar panels? I've seen them in the U.S. too.
Generating electricity with solar power, that's great!
If bicycles could also run on solar power, how cool would that be!
Many countries in the world are facing an energy crisis now. If all countries used solar and wind power, think how much oil and coal could be saved! And what's more...
Environmental protection! Haha...

I heard that the Chinese government stipulates that in offices and public spaces, the heating temperature in winter cannot exceed 20 degrees Celsius, and the air conditioning temperature in summer cannot be lower than 26 degrees.

I support this regulation with both hands. Do you remember our classrooms in the American school? In winter, we would sweat wearing just a shirt, and in summer, we'd still feel cold wearing a sweater.
Isn't that the truth! It's such a waste.
I've heard that many people now bring their own utensils when they eat out at restaurants, to avoid using disposable ones.
I think that's a great practice. Otherwise, how many trees would have to be cut down every year?
Besides bringing your own chopsticks to restaurants, you also have to bring your own bag for shopping.
Right, supermarkets in China don't give out plastic bags anymore; you have to buy them. So many people bring their own bags. I wonder how much white pollution that reduces in a year!
And then there are cars. Not only do they consume a lot of energy, but they also cause serious air pollution.
Everyone should be like us: drive less, ride bikes more, walk more. It's both environmentally friendly, energy-saving, and good for your health.
Right, the Earth is our home. We should take good care of it!
We should start with the small things, like turning off lights when we leave a room and conserving water.
Okay! Agreed!
Hey, look, we've reached the foot of the mountain. Let's start climbing. Let's see who gets to the top first! Let's go!

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 公交 is abbreviated from 公共交通.

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: 环保 is abbreviated from 环境保护.

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: 能 here is short for 能量, meaning “energy” or “power.” 能量 is often abbreviated and attached to another word indicating the source of energy, e.g., 太阳能 (solar power), 电能 (electric power), 风能 (wind power), and 水能 (hydraulic power).

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: 场所 refers to indoor or outdoor places for specific activities or purposes and generally requires a modifier, e.g. 办公场所, 休息场所, etc. By contrast, 地方 can be used on its own and can also refer to a part or aspect of someone or something, e.g. 我们有很多地方不一样(We differ in many respects).

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 摄氏 is the term for Celsius. Fahrenheit is 华氏(Huáshì). The word 氏, meaning “surname,” is often attached to the family name of a famous person or expert, here the abbreviated family names of the astronomer Anders Celsius (1701-1744) and the physicist Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit (1686-1736), respectively. As a Chinese family name, 华 is usually pronounced in the falling tone (Huà).

language_notes.label: f
language_notes.explanation_en: Here, 一次性 refers to things that are discarded after being used only once—that is, “disposable” objects.

language_notes.label: g
language_notes.explanation_en: 污 is the standard simplified form and is also frequently seen in Taiwan. Although 汙 is the official traditional form, Taiwanese newspapers use 污 instead of 汙 by a wide margin.

language_notes.label: h
language_notes.explanation_en: 节能 is abbreviated from 节约能源.

language_notes.label: i
language_notes.explanation_en: These are synonymous. 比如 is more literary than 比方说.

language_notes.label: j
language_notes.explanation_en: 都…了, like 已经…了, means “already.”

grammar_sections.title: Verb 1 的 verb 1, verb 2 的 verb 2
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Here, the 上课的 in 上课的上课 acts like a noun. Similarly, the 找工作的 in 找工作的找工作 also acts like a noun. This structure is used to imply that everyone is occupied doing his/her own thing; no one is idle.

examples.hanzi: 下班了,人们回家的回家,约朋友吃饭的约朋友吃饭,办公室很快就没有人了。
examples.pinyin: Xiàbān le, rénmen huí jiā de huí jiā, yuē péngyou chīfàn de yuē péngyou chīfàn, bàngōngshì hěn kuài jiù méiyǒu rén le.
examples.translation: After work, some went home, and some made plans to eat out with friends. The office emptied out quickly.

examples.hanzi: 运动场上学生们踢球的踢球,跑步的跑步,都在锻炼身体。
examples.pinyin: Yùndòngchǎng shàng xuéshengmen tī qiú de tī qiú, pǎobù de pǎobù, dōu zài duànliàn shēntǐ.
examples.translation: On the athletic field, some students are playing soccer and some are jogging. They're all working out.

examples.hanzi: 硬卧车厢里,旅客聊天儿的聊天儿,唱歌的唱歌,热闹极了。
examples.pinyin: Yìngwò chēxiāng lǐ, lǚkè liáotiānr de liáotiānr, chànggē de chànggē, rènào jí le.
examples.translation: In the hard-sleeper car, some passengers are chatting and some are singing. It's extremely lively.

usage_notes: This structure is used to imply that everyone is occupied doing his/her own thing; no one is idle.

grammar_sections.title: Adjective reduplication (II)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: As we learned in IC2, adjectives can be reduplicated in the form XXYY: so, the reduplicated form of the adjective 轻松 is 轻轻松松. This kind of reduplication has a heightening effect: [轻轻松松 = 很轻松]. This lesson introduces another kind of adjective reduplication. Namely, adjectives like 轻松 can be reduplicated, like verbs, in XYXY form. XYXY reduplication is used to indicate that someone wants to do something to realize the state indicated by the adjective. A reduplicated adjective in this form is sometimes preceded by the verb 让 or 叫.

patterns.meaning_en: Indicates that someone wants to do something to realize the state indicated by the adjective.

examples.hanzi: 这次考试不难, 她轻轻松松地就考了100分。
examples.translation: This time, the exam wasn't difficult. She coasted along and easily scored one hundred.

examples.hanzi: 今天是星期六, 大家都想轻松轻松。
examples.translation: Today is Saturday. Everyone wants to relax a little. [Here, 轻松轻松 indicates that everyone wants to do something in order to realize the state of being 轻松. It means “to have some fun” or “do whatever to make one feel more relaxed.”]

examples.hanzi: 洗个澡, 舒服舒服。
examples.translation: Take a bath/shower to unwind. (Here, 舒服舒服 indicates a desire to do something (i.e., take a bath/shower) in order to feel comfortable.)

examples.hanzi: 爷爷今年过八十岁生日, 我们应该好好儿地热闹热闹。
examples.translation: This year is Grandpa's eightieth birthday. We should have a big celebration and have a great time. (热闹热闹 indicates going out of one's way to make the celebration a lively, festive occasion.)

examples.hanzi: 你把这个好消息告诉他, 让他高兴高兴。
examples.translation: Tell him the good news to cheer him up. (让他高兴高兴 indicates that the person wants to make someone else happy.)

usage_notes: The XYXY form of adjective reduplication is used to indicate that someone wants to do something to realize the state indicated by the adjective.
The XYXY form is sometimes preceded by the verb 让 or 叫.

grammar_sections.title: Suggesting multiple alternatives with …吧, …吧
grammar_sections.explanation_en: This construction is usually found in spoken Chinese. It is used to indicate that the speaker is in a dilemma and unable to make a decision among the alternatives.

patterns.meaning_en: Used to express a dilemma between two or more alternatives by stating the pros and/or cons of each.

examples.hanzi: 可是怎么去呢？坐出租车吧，太贵，而且也没意思；坐公交车吧，人太多，太挤。
examples.pinyin: Kěshì zěnme qù ne? Zuò chūzūchē ba, tài guì, érqiě yě méi yìsi; zuò gōngjiāo chē ba, rén tài duō, tài jǐ.
examples.translation: But how should they get there? Taking a cab would be too expensive, and boring, too. Public transportation has too many people, and is too crowded.

examples.hanzi: 我的车旧了，最近老有问题。买新的吧，没有钱，不买新的吧，旧车又不可靠，真难办。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de chē jiù le, zuìjìn lǎo yǒu wèntí. Mǎi xīn de ba, méiyǒu qián, bù mǎi xīn de ba, jiù chē yòu bù kěkào, zhēn nán bàn.
examples.translation: My car is old, and lately it's had many problems. I could buy a new one, but I don't have the money. I could keep the old one, but it's not reliable. I don't know what to do.

examples.hanzi:
Q: 你说住在城里好，还是住在城外好？
A: 很难说。住在城里吧，上、下班，购物都很方便，可是空气不太好；住在城外吧，环境好，空气新鲜，但做什么都不太方便。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Nǐ shuō zhù zài chéng lǐ hǎo, háishì zhù zài chéngwài hǎo?
A: Hěn nán shuō. Zhù zài chéng lǐ ba, shàng, xià bān, gòuwù dōu hěn fāngbiàn, kěshì kōngqì bú tài hǎo; zhù zài chéngwài ba, huánjìng hǎo, kōngqì xīnxiān, dàn zuò shénme dōu bú tài fāngbiàn.
examples.translation:
Q: Do you think it's better to live in or outside the city?
A: It's hard to say. If you live in the city, commuting and shopping are convenient, but the air quality is not so good. If you live outside the city, the environment is good and the air is fresh, but it's quite inconvenient to do anything.

examples.hanzi: 他女朋友的妹妹明天过生日，有个生日晚会。他想，去吧，没有钱买礼物；不去吧，又怕女朋友生气。怎么办呢？
examples.pinyin: Tā nǚpéngyou de mèimei míngtiān guò shēngrì, yǒu ge shēngrì wǎnhuì. Tā xiǎng, qù ba, méiyǒu qián mǎi lǐwù; bú qù ba, yòu pà nǚpéngyou shēngqì. Zěnme bàn ne?
examples.translation: Tomorrow is his girlfriend's younger sister's birthday. There will be a birthday party in the evening. He thinks if he goes, he won't have the money to buy a present; but if he doesn't go, he's afraid that his girlfriend will be angry. What should he do?

usage_notes: This construction is usually found in spoken Chinese.
It is used to indicate that the speaker is in a dilemma and unable to make a decision among the alternatives.

grammar_sections.title: (有益)于
grammar_sections.explanation_en: The preposition 于 is generally used in formal Chinese, often after a verb. 于 has many meanings. In 有益于, 于 means 对 (to). 有益于 has the same meaning as 对…有好处 (It's good or beneficial to ...).

examples.hanzi: 大家觉得骑自行车又能锻炼身体，又省钱，而且有益于环境保护。
examples.pinyin: Dàjiā juéde qí zìxíngchē yòu néng duànliàn shēntǐ, yòu shěng qián, érqiě yǒuyì yú huánjìng bǎohù.
examples.translation: Everybody feels that biking is good exercise and saves money. Furthermore, it helps to protect the environment.

examples.hanzi: 多听录音，有益于中文学习。
examples.pinyin: Duō tīng lùyīn, yǒuyì yú Zhōngwén xuéxí.
examples.translation: Listening to recordings more is beneficial for learning Chinese.

examples.hanzi: 多听别人意见，有益于与人相处。
examples.pinyin: Duō tīng biérén yìjiàn, yǒuyì yú yǔ rén xiāngchǔ.
examples.translation: Listening to others more is helpful for getting along with people.

examples.hanzi: 体育运动有益于身体健康。
examples.pinyin: Tǐyù yùndòng yǒuyì yú shēntǐ jiànkāng.
examples.translation: Sports are good for your health.

grammar_sections.title: Adjective + 于
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Used after an adjective, 于 has the same meaning as 比. '高于 X' is synonymous with '比 X 高'. Similarly, '低于 X' has the same meaning as '比 X 低'.

patterns.meaning_en: Used to compare two things, equivalent to '比...Adjective'.

examples.hanzi: 今天的温度低于昨天。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān de wēndù dī yú zuótiān.
examples.translation: Today's temperature is lower than yesterday's.

examples.hanzi: 四大于三。
examples.translation: Four is greater than three.

examples.hanzi: 那个国家水贵于油。
examples.pinyin: Nàge guójiā shuǐ guì yú yóu.
examples.translation: In that country, water is more expensive than oil.

usage_notes: This structure is equivalent to the more common structure '比 + Noun + Adjective'. The textbook provides the following alternative phrasings for the examples: '今天的温度比昨天低。', '四比三大。', '那个国家的水比油贵。'
